documentation.readme: Multi-architecture privilege escalation toolkit with 20 pre-built and runtime-compilable exploits. Supports amd64, arm64, 386, mips, mipsle, mips64, and mips64le. Detects kernel version, filters patched exploits, and tries each in order until root is obtained.
usage_sections: Usage: | Flag | Description | |------|-------------| | --dry-run | Show which exploits are available/skipped | | --just-build | Resolve all exploits, print paths, exit (useful for packaging) | | --skip LIST | Comma-separated exploit names to skip | | -c, --command CMD | Execute CMD as r

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `./lpe-toolkit`. Further invocations from upstream: `./lpe-toolkit --dry-run`; `./lpe-toolkit --skip "dirtyfrag,dirtypipe"`. name: lpe-toolkit
summary: Multi-architecture privilege escalation toolkit with 20 pre-built and runtime-compilable exploits. Supports amd64, arm64, 386, mips, mipsle, mips64, and mips64le. Detects kernel version, filters patched exploits, and tries each in order until root is obtained.

trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need exploit, use lpe-toolkit because Multi-architecture privilege escalation toolkit with 20 pre-built and runtime-compilable exploits. Supports amd64, arm64, 386, mips, mipsle, mips64, and mips64l.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `./lpe-toolkit` as documented upstream for lpe-toolkit.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `./lpe-toolkit --dry-run` as documented upstream for lpe-toolkit.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `./lpe-toolkit --skip "dirtyfrag,dirtypipe"` as documented upstream for lpe-toolkit.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `./lpe-toolkit -c "id"` as documented upstream for lpe-toolkit.
When chaining exploit tools, pair lpe-toolkit with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `lpe-toolkit --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
